description: Ordered record of every status transition for any entity that has a lifecycle. Distinct from AuditLog — AuditLog captures what data changed, StatusHistory captures intentional state transitions with semantic context (reason, trigger). Enables lifecycle dashboards, SLA tracking, and bottleneck analysis.
consumers: any module that manages entity lifecycle — read via StatusHistory.findByEntity() and StatusHistory.currentStatus()

Id.constraints: primary key, generated by MDM routine

EntityId.constraints: id of the entity undergoing the transition

FromStatus.constraints: null on first transition (entity creation). Value must match the enum declared in the entity's own ontology

ToStatus.constraints: target status. Value must match the enum declared in the entity's own ontology

Reason.constraints: free text or enum value explaining why the transition occurred. Recommended for rejection, cancellation, and exception paths. Max 500 chars

ReasonCode.constraints: optional machine-readable code for the reason, e.g. 'BUDGET_EXCEEDED', 'SUPPLIER_INACTIVE'. Enables programmatic filtering on rejection reasons

ActorId.constraints: userId, agentId, or system service that triggered the transition

Module.constraints: lowercase module name that owns the entity

Routine.constraints: routine that triggered the transition, e.g. 'purchaseOrderApprove', 'invoiceCancel'

Metadata.constraints: optional structured context specific to the transition — e.g. approval level reached, escalation info, SLA deadline at transition time. Stored in DynamoDB only

CreatedAt.constraints: set by MDM routine at write time

postgresql.note: primary query — full lifecycle of a specific entity in order
how many entities reached this status in a period — funnel analysis
what transitions did this user trigger

dynamodb.note: time-ordered lifecycle per entity — Metadata stored here only

consistencyContract.note: index fields written synchronously — Reason and Metadata written to DynamoDB in same request

retention.postgresql: configurable per installation, default 180 days — lifecycle data has longer operational value than raw audit

derivedQueries.currentStatus: SELECT ToStatus FROM status_history WHERE EntityType = $1 AND EntityId = $2 ORDER BY CreatedAt DESC LIMIT 1
derivedQueries.avgTimeInStatus: SELECT FromStatus, AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CreatedAt - lag(CreatedAt) OVER (PARTITION BY EntityId ORDER BY CreatedAt)))) FROM status_history WHERE EntityType = $1 AND Module = $2 GROUP BY FromStatus
derivedQueries.note: these query templates are referenced by the HDM KPI layer — do not move business logic here
